result.justification: The patient is an 80-year-old female with documented confusion and fatigue with reported increased difficulty with balance and use of a walker at home (ED note 10/27/2019, RN notes 11/23-11/24/2019). She had a recent syncope episode without fall but with risk of fall (ED note 11/22/2019, RN notes 11/23/2019). She is on aspirin 324 mg (medication list 10/27/2019) which increases bleeding risk. Labs show hypokalemia (potassium 2.3 on 11/22/2019 and 3.2 on 10/27/2019) and acute kidney injury (creatinine elevated to 1.64 on 10/27/2019 and worsening on 11/22/2019). She is on prednisone (up to 60 mg daily) and Bactrim DS for PCP prophylaxis, indicating immunosuppression (medications and ED notes). Family reports forgetfulness and vivid dreams causing confusion (ED note 10/27/2019). These factors combined increase risks for falls, bleeding, infection, and medication adherence challenges. No history of contrast reaction or implanted devices was reported (MRI screening form). These risks are important for ongoing management including fall precautions, monitoring electrolytes and renal function, infection prevention, and careful medication review.
result.medical_risks: Fall propensity, cognitive impairment/confusion, bleeding risk from aspirin use, hypokalemia, acute kidney injury, immunosuppression from prednisone and Bactrim use, syncope risk

result.justification: Marital status is documented as married in multiple notes including ED and oncology consults (06/2019, 07/2019). Living situation is described as living with family and husband in PT and social work notes (11/2019). Functional barriers are detailed in physical therapy notes (11/2019) describing need for assistance with ADLs, use of walker, and impaired balance and endurance. Financial insecurity and food insecurity are explicitly documented as none in social history from ED notes (07/2019) and nutrition assessment (11/2019). Insurance status is documented as Medicare A and B and Clark Co Self Funded in social work notes (11/2019). Mental health concerns including depression and anxiety are noted in consultant and social history reviews but no formal screening scores are present. Education level and employment status are not documented. Transportation and housing insecurity are not documented. Health literacy and utility insecurity are not documented.
result.mental_health: Depression documented in review of systems; anxiety and confusion noted in clinical notes; no standardized screening scores documented

_items.Justification: Document 74 is an ophthalmology note dated 2019-04-22 describing detailed lens findings, cataract types, dry eye syndrome, and surgical planning including YAG capsulotomy and cataract surgery with intraocular lens, indicating a preoperative ophthalmic evaluation.
_items.notes: Lens exam showed 1+ PCO, 2+ NS, 1+ cortical cataract, and 1+ PSC; angles open; dry eye syndrome noted; patient planned for cataract surgery with intraocular lens implantation and YAG capsulotomy for PCO.

_items.Justification: Document 20 is a post-operative ophthalmology note dated 2019-05-03 describing day 1 post cataract surgery OS, normal tear film, optic nerve assessment, glaucoma suspect status, and intraocular lens presence, confirming a post-op follow-up visit.
_items.notes: Post-op day 1 cataract surgery left eye with expected healing; normal tear film; cup-to-disc ratio 40; flat periphery; open angle glaucoma suspect right eye; presence of intraocular lens in both eyes.

_items.Justification: Document 77 is an ophthalmology/optometry encounter dated 2024-06-09 describing bilateral eye redness, conjunctival injection, no discharge, normal pupils and fundus, diagnosis of blepharoconjunctivitis, and treatment prescribed, indicating an acute eye problem evaluation.
_items.notes: Bilateral conjunctival injection with redness and itchiness; no discharge; pupils equal and reactive; funduscopic exam normal without hemorrhage; diagnosed with blepharoconjunctivitis and treated with olopatadine and polymyxin B sulf-trimethoprim eye drops.

result.justification: Information was extracted from multiple clinical notes including ED attestation (06/08/2019), hematology consults (07/23/2019, 10/28/2019), nursing plans of care (2019), physical therapy assessment (11/24/2019), nutrition assessment (11/24/2019), and procedural notes (07/15/2020).
result.other_pertinent_information: The patient is an 80-year-old female with a longstanding history of chronic autoimmune hemolytic anemia diagnosed in 2012, status post splenectomy, who remains transfusion-dependent despite treatment with steroids, rituximab, and azathioprine. She has developed complications including iron overload requiring chelation therapy, iatrogenic Cushing's syndrome from steroid use, and worsening dementia possibly related to iron overload or Alzheimer's disease. The patient has undergone multiple hospital admissions for severe anemia requiring frequent red blood cell transfusions complicated by warm autoantibodies making blood matching difficult. She has a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, hysterectomy, hernia repair, and right shoulder surgery, and has a port for frequent blood draws which was removed in 2020. Recent evaluations include physical therapy for impaired mobility and balance, nutrition assessment for inadequate intake and weight loss, and echocardiogram showing normal ejection fraction. She lives at home with her husband and has family support, including a daughter who is a nurse and assists with care. Recent preventive screenings include a colonoscopy in 2013 and mammogram status is not explicitly documented.

result.justification: Immunizations were extracted from the Immunization Record tables documented in laboratory and clinical summary documents dated 2019 and 2018, explicitly listing administered vaccines with dates. The Tdap vaccine was confirmed administered on 2018-10-15 from a primary care note. Multiple influenza vaccines, Hepatitis A, Pneumococcal, and Zoster vaccines were documented with administration dates, not as due or declined.
